_about: 100 fabricated bedtime stories with known world labels — the world-classifier benchmark. Synthetic (no family content), so committable. Gold = world_gold/world_aliases/bucket/difficulty; the runner (bench_worlds.py) reads only id+lines.

lines: Okay so once there was a boy named Luke who lived on a desert planet with two suns.
Two suns? In the sky at the same time?
Yep, two of 'em, big orange ones. And he wanted to be a Jedi like his dad.
And then he meets the old man, Obi-Wan, and the little blue robot.
R2-D2! And the gold one that talks too much.
Right, C-3PO. And Obi-Wan gives him a lightsaber and teaches him about the Force.
Wait, what's the Force again?
It's like an energy that flows through everything, and the Jedi can use it to move stuff and, you know, stuff.
And then the bad guy Darth Vader, with the black helmet and the breathing.
He's so scary. Do the breathing.
I'm not doing the breathing, lie back down.
So Luke and Han Solo and Chewbacca fly the Millennium Falcon to go save Princess Leia.
And they blow up the Death Star with the X-wings!
Yes, Luke uses the Force to make the shot and the whole space station goes boom.
And Vader was actually his dad the whole time, but that's the next one.
No more tonight, okay? Goodnight.

lines: This one's about the little green guy in the swamp, remember him?
Yoda!
Yeah, Yoda. So Luke crashes his ship in the swamp on the planet Dagobah.
And his ship sinks down in the mud and he's all upset.
And the little green Jedi master is like, hmm, do or do not, there is no try.
He talks backwards funny.
He does. And he tells Luke to lift the ship out of the swamp with the Force.
With his mind?
With his mind. Luke can't do it, but Yoda just closes his eyes and floats the whole ship up.
Whoa.
And Luke's like, how, and Yoda goes, size matters not, the Force is everywhere.
Then Luke leaves to go fight Darth Vader even though Yoda says he's not ready.
And Vader cuts off his hand! With the red lightsaber!
Yep, and tells him the big secret. But we're stopping there.
Aww. One more part?
Nope, eyes closed, may the Force be with you.

lines: Okay tonight it's the one with the little green baby and the guy in the silver armor.
The baby with the big ears!
That's him. So the bounty hunter wears this shiny helmet, never takes it off.
Why doesn't he take it off?
It's his whole thing, it's a rule for his people. Anyway he gets a job to go find the little one.
But when he finds the baby he can't hand him over, so he steals him back.
And the baby can lift things with his mind, right?
Right, he reaches out his little hand and a big creature just floats up in the air.
And everybody's chasing them across all these planets in the ship.
His little spaceship that he flies, yeah.
Mm-hm. And there's the lady who fights and the big bug guys after the bounty.
Then the mean guys in the gray uniforms want the baby for, um, experiments.
Because of the magic energy in his blood?
Something like that. And one of 'em calls the little guy Grow-goo, but I think he says it wrong.
Grow-goo!
Anyway the guy in armor swears to protect him no matter what, like a dad now. Okay, that's enough, scoot down.

lines: So there's a girl who lives all alone on a sandy planet, scavenging old broken ships for parts.
Like a junkyard?
A giant junkyard desert, yeah. And one day a little round robot rolls up to her, beeping.
A round one? Not the tall ones?
Round, like a ball that rolls, with a little head on top. And it's got a secret map inside it.
A map to what?
To find the last old Jedi, the one who disappeared. Everybody's looking for him.
And the bad guys want the map too, the ones in the black uniforms.
And the guy with the cracked red mask, the one who's scary and angry all the time.
He can stop a laser blast in midair just by holding up his hand.
Whoa, freeze it in the air?
Yep, freezes it, just floating there. He's strong with the dark side.
And the girl, she's never used the power before, but she starts to feel it.
She lifts a sword to her hand without touching it!
Right, it flies across the snow into her grip. And later the bad guy goes by Kyle, Kylo, something like that. But that's where we stop tonight.
No fair, it was just getting good.

lines: Lie down, okay. So tonight it's the two brothers who built things together.
The smiths!
Yeah, they were kind of like mechanics, they could fix any machine in the whole galaxy.
And then a war came and they had to pick sides.
One brother stayed good, and one brother got tricked by the man in the dark robe.
The one with the scratchy whispery voice?
That one. He promised the brother he could save the people he loved if he joined him.
And he believed him?
He did, and it was a lie, and it turned him mean and burned him up inside.
So they had to fight each other on a river of fire, the two brothers.
On lava?
Right by it, jumping around on the rocks over the lava, real dangerous. It was on this place, um, Anny-kin called it home, the burny planet.
And the good one wins, but he doesn't kill his brother, he just leaves him there.
That's so sad.
It is. And the bad brother gets put in the black suit so he can breathe. Okay, sleep.
Wait, is that the breathing guy from before?
Eyes closed, we'll talk tomorrow.

lines: Okay, quick one. There's a girl whose whole job is stealing the secret plans.
Plans for what?
For the giant weapon, the round one that can blow up a whole world with one shot.
A whole world? At once?
All of it, just gone, in one big green beam. That's why the plans mattered so much.
So she gets a team together, and there's a blind man who fights amazing with just a stick.
A blind guy who can fight?
The best of 'em. He keeps whispering this little prayer to himself about the power, like a chant.
And there's a grumpy robot, a tall one that says exactly what it's thinking.
Rude robot!
Super rude, yeah. And they sneak onto a beach planet with palm trees to go grab the plans.
Palm trees in space?
Tropical, with the ocean and everything. I think it's called Scary-f, the beach one. And they fight on the sand to send the plans up to the ship.
Do they make it?
They get the plans out, but the team, um. They don't come home.
They die?
Everybody, yeah. But because of them the good guys win later. Okay, that's it, goodnight.

lines: Okay so tonight it's the one about He-Man, the strongest man in the universe.
He-Man! Like the muscle guy?
Yeah that's him. By day he's really Prince Adam, kind of a lazy prince.
But when there's trouble he holds up his sword and yells, by the power of Grayskull!
And then ZAP, he turns into He-Man.
And his scaredy-cat Cringer turns into a big green tiger called Battle Cat.
Can I ride the tiger?
Maybe in a dream, lie back down. So the bad guy is Skeletor.
He's got a skull for a face and a glowy staff.
Spooky! Why is his face a skull?
Nobody knows, that's just Skeletor. And he's always trying to break into Castle Grayskull.
'Cause inside is the biggest secret power in all of Eternia.
So Skeletor sneaks up with his goons, and He-Man and Teela and Man-At-Arms gotta stop him.
They fight and He-Man just picks up a whole boulder and throws it.
Whoa. Does Skeletor win?
Never. He always runs away yelling, I'll get you next time, He-Man!
And everybody laughs and the castle's safe again.
Okay, that's the end. Eyes closed now.

lines: You wanted the Castle Grayskull one tonight, right?
Yes! The green castle with the face!
Right, the big stone face, you walk right through the mouth.
So the Sorceress lives in there, she guards all the magic of Eternia.
And one day Skeletor and his mean pal Evil-Lyn try to take the castle.
Skeletor's the one with the skull face. He's so mean.
He is. So the Sorceress sends a secret message to Prince Adam.
And Adam grabs his sword and shouts, by the power of Grayskull, I have the power!
And then what, then what?
Then he becomes He-Man, the most powerful man in the universe.
His cat Cringer gets scared, but Adam zaps him into Battle Cat too.
I love Battle Cat.
Me too. And Teela and Man-At-Arms ride out to help.
They all charge the bad guys and He-Man bends Skeletor's staff in half like a pretzel.
Ha! A pretzel!
So Skeletor poofs away in a cloud of purple smoke, all mad.
And Grayskull's safe, and Eternia's safe, and you're safe in bed.
The end. Night night, my little hero.

lines: So there's this prince, Prince Adam, and everybody thinks he's kind of lazy.
Lazy how?
Like he naps a lot and nobody knows his big secret.
His secret is, when there's danger, he lifts his magic sword way up high.
And he shouts the power words and he changes into the strongest hero around.
His pet cat's a big scaredy-cat, until the magic turns him into a giant battle tiger.
So one night the bone-faced villain Skellator sneaks toward the old castle.
Skellator? That's a weird name.
Yeah, the recorder heard it kinda funny. He's got a skull head and a glowing staff.
He wants the power locked inside that big green castle on the hill.
So the prince and the captain's daughter ride out to stop him.
The captain's daughter is a warrior too, she's real brave.
They cross the whole kingdom of Eternia to get there in time.
And there's a big fight, and the hero throws the villain right off the wall.
Does the skull guy come back?
Always does. He floats away promising to come back. But the castle's secret stays safe.
And the prince goes back to pretending he's just sleepy old Adam.
Okay. Sleepy like you. Lie down.

lines: Tonight the hero's cat is the star, okay? His name's Crinjer.
A cat hero?
Well, half the time he's a total coward, hides behind the furniture.
But when the hero raises his glowing sword, the cat gets blasted with the same magic.
And he turns into this huge armored tiger you can actually ride into battle.
Ride a tiger! Yes!
Right? So one day an alarm goes off across the whole land of Eternia.
The bony bad guy's marching an army at the gray castle again.
So the hero and the tiger and the captain's daughter charge out the gates.
Was the captain's daughter scared?
Never. She's the best fighter in the royal guard. Her dad makes all the cool gadgets.
They smash through the goons, and the tiger pounces right on the skull-faced wizard.
And the hero grabs him and tosses him in the moat.
Splash! Bad guy soup!
Ha, exactly. He swims off swearing he'll be back.
And the cat shrinks back into a little coward and hides under the throne again.
And everybody scratches his ears and calls him a good boy.
The end, sweet pea.

lines: So once there was a prince who acted lazy on purpose so nobody guessed his secret.
What was the secret?
When trouble came he'd hold up an old sword and shout some magic words.
And he'd change into someone way stronger, way more muscles.
But during the day he just yawned and let everybody think he was useless.
That's kinda funny.
It is. His best helper made all the inventions and weapons for the palace.
One night a wizard with a glowing staff tried to sneak into the secret stronghold.
The stronghold was carved to look like a giant skull, you walk in through the teeth.
Creepy!
Very. The prince said the magic words and went out to defend it.
Didn't even need a weapon, he just lifted the whole gate off its hinges.
And his friend, the big tiger he rides, his name was something like Cringe-er.
Cringe-er, ha.
Yeah, the recorder heard it funny. So the wizard threw his magic but it bounced right off.
So the wizard gave up and zoomed off into the dark sky.
And the prince walked home and went right back to acting sleepy.
So nobody ever knew the lazy prince was the mighty hero.
Like a secret. I can keep secrets.
I know you can. Eyes closed, keeper of secrets.

lines: Okay so there's this boy at a boarding school up in a big stone castle.
And the school's secret, because, well, everybody there's a witch or a wizard.
Like with wands?
With wands, yeah, and the boy's best friend's a girl who reads everything, um, Her My Own E.
She's so smart she fixes everything they break.
And there's this huge friendly groundskeeper with a tangled beard, Hag Rid, keeps all these strange animals.
And they have flying lessons up on broomsticks over the courtyard.
And a game in the air, four balls, you've gotta catch the tiny gold one to win.
Did the boy win?
He's the best flier in the whole school, even though he's the youngest one.
And an owl brings his mail right to the table at breakfast, swoops down.
Why an owl?
That's just how the letters come there. Now scoot under the covers.
And the three friends sneak out at night to figure out the castle's big secret.

lines: There was once this skinny kid who lived with his awful aunt and uncle.
They made him sleep in a closet under the stairs, can you believe that.
That's so mean.
But on his birthday a letter came, and then like a hundred letters, then a thousand.
And they took him off to a castle school where he learned to do magic.
The school's called Hog Warts, funny name, right.
Hog warts, like a pig?
Ha, yeah. And the headmaster's this gentle old man, Dumble Door, knows everything.
And there was a bad sorcerer everyone was scared of, they wouldn't even say his name.
They just called him You-Know-Who.
Spooky.
And the kid had a scar on his forehead, shaped like lightning, from when he was a baby.
Okay, last sip of milk, then eyes closed.
And he made two best friends and they had adventures all year long.

lines: So there's this boy away at a school where they're all learning special things.

And another friend, freckly, lots of brothers.

Very. Calm, twinkly eyes, kind of always seems to know what you're about to ask.

And there's somebody out there, in the dark, that the grownups are all frightened of.
Are the kids scared?
A little. But they're brave. Okay, that's enough, lie back down now.
And they figure it out together, the three of them. Sleep tight.

lines: Tonight let's do the one about the kids in the castle, the four houses one.
Each kid gets sorted into a house when they get there, and ours is the brave one.
Which house?
Grif and Door, the lions, red and gold. And the sneaky ones are the snakes.
So the boy and his two friends are in the brave house.
And there's a teacher in black robes who really doesn't like the boy, just glares at him.
Why doesn't he like him?
It's a long story, we'll get to it another night. Anyway.
And they have this big feast in a hall where the ceiling looks like the sky.
Like outside?
Like the real night sky, with candles floating up near it. It's pretty.
And they win points for their house all year by doing good and being brave.
Okay, that's the chapter. Under the covers.
And at the very end the brave house wins the cup. Goodnight, love.

lines: Okay so tonight it's Paw Patrol, the one where the pups save the day, you ready?
Yeah! Chase and Marshall!
Right, so Ryder gets the call on his big watch thing, beep beep beep.
And he says no job is too big, no pup is too small.
Then what happens?
So all the puppies run up the slide into the big tower, the lookout.
Chase is the police one, he goes ready for action Ryder sir.
And Marshall is the firetruck one and he trips on the stairs like always.
Why does he always trip?
Because he's a little clumsy, that's why he's funny.
And Skye is the pink one, she flies in her helicopter, whoosh.
Rubble! He digs!
Yes, Rubble has the bulldozer, he likes to dig holes.
So a kitty got stuck up a tree in Adventure Bay.
And Mayor Goodway is all worried about her chicken Chickaletta.
So Chase and Skye go up and get the kitty down safe.
And everybody barks yay, mission complete.
The end, lie back down now.
Can we do another one?
No, one pup story, it's bedtime.

Bawk!
And everybody cheers and they eat festival pie.

lines: Okay last one, lie down. There's a town by the sea and a boy who helps people.
He's not a grownup, he's like a big kid, and he rides a quad bike everywhere.
And he's got a team that comes when things go wrong.
What kind of team?
Helpers, with little trucks, each one does a different job, you know that.
So one morning the train was coming and a tree fell on the tracks.
And the conductor couldn't stop in time, choo choo, getting close.
Uh oh, what did they do?
So the boy taps his wrist gadget and says, alright everybody, to the tower.
And the strong one, the build pup, um, Ruddle I think, he brings the wrecking claw.
And the police helper set out the orange cones and the net first.
Then they lift the big tree right off the rails.
Then what?
They cleared the whole tree just before the train got there, screech, it stopped.
And the conductor waved thank you out the window.
And the boy goes, see, when you work together you can do anything.
Was anybody hurt?
Nope, everybody safe, even the squirrels in the tree.
Okay, that's the end, goodnight, no more questions.

lines: You want the spotty dog one? Okay, scoot over.
So there's this puppy and he's white with black spots all over.
And he's a fire dog, he rides the red truck with the ladder.
Like a dalmatian?
Yeah like that, but he's clumsy, he always trips coming down the pole.
His name's kind of like a swamp, Marsh-something.
Marsh-all?
That's it. So the school bell tower had a fire alarm going off, beep beep.
And it was just a cat that knocked something over, no real fire.
But the spotty pup didn't know, so he rolls up with the hose and the ladder.
And he sprays a little bit and trips over his own paws, splash.
Did he get wet?

lines: Okay so tonight it's the Octonauts, the one with the whale.
The Octonauts live in the Octopod, that big white underwater base shaped like an octopus.
Captain Barnacles is the polar bear, he's the captain, and he's very brave.
And Kwazii is the orange kitten pirate, he says "yarr" all the time.
Yeah Kwazii's the one with the eye patch, good memory.
And then there's Peso, the little penguin, he's the medic.
Peso gets scared but he's still really brave, that's the whole thing about Peso.
One day they got a call, a humpback whale was tangled up in an old net.
Why was the net there? People leave junk in the ocean, that's why.
So Captain Barnacles took the Gup-A and zoomed down to help.
Kwazii used his sword to cut the net, snip snip.
And Peso checked the whale to make sure she wasn't hurt.
Then the whale was free and she sang a big whale song, woooo.
Octonauts, let's do this! That's what they say before a mission.
And at the end, Tweak the rabbit fixed up the Gups.
Explore, rescue, protect — okay, lie back down please.
Goodnight, my little Octonaut.

Giant squid, lives in the deep sea, has the biggest eyes of any animal.
Okay that's it, the Octonauts went back home to the Octopod.
Night night, sleep tight.

lines: Lie down, I'll tell you the one with the sea bunnies.
So the captain, the big white bear, he got a message early in the morning.
Something was glowing way out on the reef, all this blue light.
He woke up Kazzy — the orange one, the cat — and the little penguin doctor.
Drink your water first. Okay.
They climbed into the little submarines, the Gups, and shot out of the base.
The base is shaped like a big sea creature with eight arms, you know the one.
Down they went past the coral, deeper and deeper.
And the glowing was sea bunnies! Tiny little slugs, kind of, with little ears.
No they're real, they're called sea bunnies, look it up.
The cat one wanted to grab one but the bear said no, just look, don't touch.
Then the current got strong and swept the penguin away.
But they caught him with the sub's claw, phew.
And the captain played his accordion to keep everybody calm.
Yeah he plays the accordion, the bear captain does.
Then they did the little report about the animal and went home.
The end. Eyes closed.

lines: Okay one more, the one about the spooky fish with the light.
So the crew's all asleep in the big underwater pod thing.
And Pesso, the little penguin in the medic hat, he can't sleep, he's nervous.
Why's he nervous? He's always a little nervous, that's just Pesso.
Then there's a thump on the hull, thump thump, in the dark.
The orange pirate kitten grabs his little sword, ready to fight.
No, no, stay in bed, I'm right here.
They take a Gup out — that's the little sub — to go look.
And out in the black water there's two glowing dots coming closer.
It's an anglerfish! The one with the little fishing-rod light on its head.
The light tricks other fish so the anglerfish can eat them, sneaky.
Pesso was scared but he checked the fish anyway, that's brave.
And it turned out the anglerfish just had a sore fin.
So the penguin fixed it up with a little bandage.
Then they all swam back to the pod and tucked in.
See? Even scary fish just need a little help sometimes.
Goodnight.

lines: Bedtime story. The one with the crab who lost his shell.
So there's this whole little crew that lives at the bottom of the sea.
They've got a round home base and they zip around in tiny submarines.
One of them, Kuwazee, he's got an eye patch and talks like a pirate.
The rest of the crew was busy so Kuwazee went out alone.
And he found a hermit crab with no shell, all soft and worried.
Why no shell? Hermit crabs borrow shells, and his got too small, he grew.
So they swam around the seafloor looking for a new shell to fit him.
Found an old snail shell, too big. Found a bottle cap, too hard.
Then a perfect empty whelk shell, just right, and the crab backed right in.
Kuwazee did a little flip, he was so happy.
Then he wrote down notes about the crab, like a little nature report.
Lie back down, almost done.
And he steered his sub home through the dark water.
The other animals were waiting with a warm bowl of something the little cooks made.
And everybody went to sleep at the bottom of the sea.
The end, close your eyes.

lines: Okay, the calm one tonight, the sleepy whale shark.
There's a captain, a big polar bear, leads a little undersea rescue team.
He plays a squeezebox, the accordion, when things get tense.
Don't kick your sister. Okay, go on.
One night the sonar picked up something huge moving slow in the dark.
The captain — Barnnacles is his name — took out the little sub by himself.
And it was a whale shark, the biggest fish in the whole ocean, but gentle.
It only eats teeny tiny things, plankton, even though it's enormous.
The whale shark was sleepy and kept bumping into the coral.
So the bear captain guided it out to open water, real slow and patient.
He hummed along on his squeezebox so the shark would stay calm.
Then he did his little log about the animal, the way he always does.
Why's he write it down? So he remembers every creature he meets.
And he turned the sub around and went back to the round home base.
Hung up his captain's hat and went to bed.
Night, sweetheart. Sleep like a whale shark.

lines: Okay tonight it's the one about Hilda, the girl with the blue hair, remember her?
The one who lives in the wilderness with her mom and her little deerfox?
Yeah the deerfox is named Twig. He's like a fox but he's got these tiny little antlers.
And then what?
So Hilda's out drawing the mountains and a giant wakes up, a big lonely giant.
But then they gotta move into the city, into Trolberg, because of the trolls.
Why are the trolls bad?
They're not all bad, baby, they just come down from the mountains at night.
There's a big wall around Trolberg with bells, and the bells warn everybody.
And Hilda makes friends, David and Frida, and they join the Sparrow Scouts.
Is that like real scouts?
Sort of, with badges and everything, but they meet weird woodmen and elves too.
There's a tiny elf, a whole invisible elf kingdom, you gotta sign paperwork to even see them.
That's silly.
It is silly. Now lie back down, Twig the deerfox is already asleep.
Can we see the nisse next time? The one who lives in the empty space?
Yes, the nisse, next time. Eyes closed now.

lines: So there's this big old stone abbey out in the woods and all the mice live there.
The mice live in a church?
Sort of an abbey, yeah, big great hall, and they have these huge feasts, cordial and pies and all that.
And there's a young mouse, kind of clumsy, his name's Mathias.
He wants to be brave like the old warrior mouse from the painting on the wall.
Then what happens?
Then this evil rat shows up, a one-eyed rat, Clooney the Scourge they call him, with a whole army, rats and weasels and stoats.
And they want the abbey?
They want the abbey and they want to eat up all the food.
So Mathias goes off looking for the lost sword, the great warrior's sword.
Is the sword magic?
Not magic, no, just real old and real sharp, hidden way up high where a sparrow king lives.
And the badgers and the otters and the hares help defend the walls.
The hares talk funny, 'jolly good, wot wot,' all that.
Drink your milk, c'mon, before it gets warm.
So the clumsy little mouse turns into a real warrior, the rats get beaten, and everybody feasts again.
I like the feast part.
Everybody likes the feast part. Okay. Sleep.

lines: Want the one in the valley with the little round white fellow?
The one that looks like a hippo but isn't a hippo?
Right, him. So it's the very end of autumn and everybody's getting ready to sleep all winter.
Why do they sleep all winter?
Cause that's just what they do, they eat pine needles and curl up till spring.
But his friend in the green hat won't sleep, he's packing up his tent and his little mouth organ.
Where's he going?
South, on his own, like he does every year. Says he'll be back when the first warm wind comes.
And the little white fellow's sad his friend's leaving, Snuffkin, his name's Snuffkin.
So he gives him a present?
No, his friend just plays one last song down by the bridge and walks off whistling.
And the mamma, the one with the apron and the big handbag, she tucks everyone in.
Is the pappa there?
Yeah, the pappa's off writing his memoirs again, very important, he keeps saying.
Then it snows and snows and the whole valley goes quiet and white.
And he wakes up?
He wakes up one time too early, middle of winter, but that's a whole other night. Sleep now.

lines: Tonight it's the boy from the island, the one who's so good at magic he gets cocky.
Cocky means?
Means he shows off. The other young wizards at the school dare him to.
So he does this big spell he isn't ready for, tries to call up a spirit from the dead.
And then?
And this shadow thing tears out of the dark and claws his face and gets loose in the world.
And it chases him?
For years. Over the sea, island to island, in his little boat with the red sail.
Why doesn't he just hide?
Cause you can't hide from your own shadow, sweetheart. It's wearing his face.
His teacher told him everything's got a secret name, a true name, and if you know a thing's true name you have power over it.
Even a dragon?
Even a dragon. He met one, this old golden dragon out on the burnt islands, and he didn't even flinch. The dragon kept calling him by some other name, Spar-hawk, Sparrowhawk, something like that.
How does he beat the shadow?
Right at the very edge of the world, out on the open water, he turns around and faces it and says a name.
Whose name?
His own. The shadow had his name the whole time. Okay, that's enough now, eyes shut.

lines: Okay, the young reporter, the one with the little tuft of hair and the white dog.
The dog that talks?
He doesn't really talk, he just thinks stuff and we hear it, his name's Snowy.
So the reporter and Snowy are chasing some crooks who stole something off a ship.
And the grumpy sailor guy helps?
Captain Hadock, yeah, the bearded captain, always shouting these funny insults.
Like what?
'Billions of blistering blue barnacles!' and 'thundering typhoons!', that kind of thing.
And he likes his whisky way too much, but never mind about that part.
Then the two detectives turn up, the ones in the matching bowler hats with the canes.
Tomson and Thomson?
Right, and they say everything twice and get it completely wrong every time.
What did the crooks steal?
A clue to a sunken treasure, some old scroll, so they all sail off to go find it.
Does the deaf professor come?
Professor Calculus with his little pendulum, yeah, can never hear a word anybody says.

lines: Okay so once upon a time there was a man named He-Man, the strongest man in the whole universe.
He lived on a planet called Eternia. Eternia, yeah.
And he had a magic sword, and when he held it up he yelled 'I have the power!'
Then what happened?
Then he turned from regular Prince Adam into He-Man, big muscles and everything.
And there was a bad guy, a really scary one, with a skull for a face.
Skeletor?
Yes, Skeletor, with his blue skin and his glowy yellow staff.
Skeletor wanted to break into Castle Grayskull to steal all the secrets inside.
But guarding the castle was a brand new friend, a little rock-creature named Pebblo who could roll into a boulder.
Is Pebblo real? In the cartoon?
No bug, Pebblo is one we made up, just for tonight.
So Pebblo rolled down the hill and knocked Skeletor's evil army over like bowling pins.
And then He-Man came charging in on Battle Cat, the big green tiger.
Roar!
Exactly. And Skeletor shouted 'I'll get you next time, He-Man!' and ran away to Snake Mountain.
And Pebblo got a medal for being so brave.
Okay, lie back down now, the story's done.
One more part!
No, that's the end. Goodnight, kiddo.

lines: Alright, the Harry Potter one, like you asked, but a new version.
So Harry and his friends are back at Hogwarts for another year.
Ron and Her-my-own-ee too?
Yep, Ron and Hermione, all three of them.
And this year there's a new kid in Gryffindor named Tilly Sparrowmoor.
Tilly's not in the books, right?
Right, Tilly's just ours, made her up.
So Tilly is amazing at flying, even better than Harry at Quidditch.
And one night they sneak down to the kitchens past Filch.
And the headmaster, old Dumble-door with his long white beard, catches them.
Uh oh.
But he just smiles and gives them lemon drops and sends them to bed.
Then the bad guy, you-know-who, Voldemort, sends a cursed letter to the school.
And Tilly's the one who figures out it's a trap, with her clever spell.
What spell?
A protection charm she invented herself, lit up the whole Great Hall gold.
And Gryffindor won the house cup because of her.
The end. Eyes closed.
Can Tilly be in the next one too?
Sure, Tilly can come back tomorrow night. Sleep.

lines: Okay tonight it's the strong guy with the sword again, the one who turns into the hero.
The prince?
Yeah, the prince who lifts his sword up and gets all his power and becomes the big strong one.
So there's trouble at the old castle, the one shaped like a giant skull on the hill.
And the bony bad guy, Skellator, he's plotting again with that purple hood of his.
He wants to get inside the skull castle and take the magic.
But this time he's got a new helper, a sneaky frost-witch named Madame Cindara who freezes anyone she touches.
Is she scary?
A little, but our hero's not afraid.
He hops on his big armored cat and rides up the mountain.
And Madame Cindara freezes the drawbridge solid so he can't get across.
Then what'd he do?
He smashed the ice with one swing and yelled out his power words.
And Skellator's whole plan fell apart, and he ran back to his snake-shaped mountain.
And Madame Cindara melted into a puddle and floated away down the river.
Did the cat get the bad guys?
The cat got 'em. Big roar, sent 'em running.
Okay, that's enough, drink the last of your milk and lie down.
Mmkay.
Night night.

lines: So the boy wizard with the round glasses and the scar on his forehead, you know him.
He goes back to the big castle school for wizards on the train.
And his two best friends come too, the red-haired boy and the smart girl, Her-my-own-ee.
And there's a new teacher this year, Professor Quillby, who teaches a class about talking to animals.
Professor who?
Quillby, he's new, we made him up. He's got an owl that sits on his shoulder all day.
Anyway, somebody steals a magic mirror from the trophy room.
And everyone blames the boy and his friends.
But it wasn't them!
Nope, wasn't them. So they sneak out at night under the invisible cloak to find the real thief.
And they catch Professor Quillby's owl carrying the mirror in its claws.
The owl did it?
The owl was being controlled by a dark spell, from the bad wizard who never says his real name.
And the kids broke the spell with their wands and got the house points back.
Did the owl get in trouble?
No, the owl was just bewitched, poor thing. They gave it some treats.
And the smart girl figured the whole thing out before anybody else, like always.
Okay, that's the end. Snuggle in.
Wands are cool.
They are. Goodnight.

lines: Once there was a quiet prince who had a great big secret.
When his kingdom was in danger he'd raise up his glowing sword and say the magic words.
And then?
And then he changed, taller and stronger, the bravest warrior on the whole world of, um, Eeternya I think she calls it.
He had a friend too, a clumsy royal cook named Bartholomew Stew who burned everything he touched.
Bartholomew Stew, that's a funny name.
He made it up himself because he was embarrassed about the burning, that's the joke.
Anyway, one day a swarm of metal beetles came marching toward the palace.
Were they robots?
Sort of, evil little machine bugs sent from the dark mountain far away.
And Bartholomew Stew accidentally dumped a whole pot of hot soup on them.
And the beetles all short-circuited and went pfffft.
Then the prince raised his sword and finished the rest of them.
And the kingdom was safe and they had a big feast.
Did Bartholomew get to cook the feast?
He did, and for once nothing burned.
Okay, eyes shut now.
Was the prince the strongest in the universe?
The strongest there ever was. Now sleep, love.

lines: Okay okay lie down, you ready? So once there was a big emergency in Adventure Bay.
And Ryder pushed the button and he called all the pups, no don't sit up.
He said no job is too big, no pup is too small!
Then what?
So Chase came zooming up in his police truck, and Marshall the fire dog right behind him.
Skye flew her little helicopter, whoosh, up in the sky.
Why did Ryder call them?
Because a kitten got stuck way up on the lighthouse, that's why.
But here's the new part, okay? There was a brand new pup named Sprocket.
Sprocket is a tiny dog who can fix anything with his magic wrench.
Is Sprocket real?
He's our pretend one, yeah, we made him up. He's the best builder pup.
So Sprocket built a little ladder right up to the kitten.
And Chase used his net and Marshall sprayed water to make it soft.
Then they all rescued the kitty and Ryder said great job!
And Sprocket got his very own pup tag, a shiny gold wrench.
The end. Drink your water.
Can Sprocket come tomorrow night too?

lines: So down in the deep deep ocean there's the team that lives in the Octopod.
What's the Octopod?
It's their underwater base, shaped like an octopus, with all the little submarines.
And the captain is a big polar bear, Captain Barnacles, he's so brave.
And there's a pirate cat with one eye, Kwazee, he loves adventures.
Kwazee always says yo ho ho.
So one day a baby creature was crying in a cave.
And tonight we put in a new one, a glowing seahorse named Lumi.
Lumi can light up the whole dark sea with her tail.
Did the captain find her?
Yeah, Barnacles took the little sub down to the cave.
And Kwazee swam in and Lumi lit everything up so they could see.
The crying baby was a lost octopus pup, all tangled up in seaweed.
So they cut him free and brought him back to his mama.
And then they did the creature report like they always do.
Lumi stayed to be the new light of the sea. Okay, eyes closed.

A train was stuck on the tracks with rocks all over it.
So he called the bulldog, Rubble, the one who drives the digger.
Rubble, Rubble on the double! That's what he says.
And the police one in the blue, he set up the cones with his net.
But the rocks were too heavy for just them.
So tonight there's a new pup, an ox-strong one named Tank.
Tank is a huge gentle pup who can pull anything, even a train.
Is Tank stronger than Rubble?
Way stronger, but Rubble digs faster. They're a team.
So Tank pulled and Rubble dug and they got the rocks off the tracks.
And the train went choo choo, all the people clapped.
Rider gave Tank a new tag and said good work, pup.
Okay that's it, lie back down for real now.

lines: So it was snowing really hard up on the big mountain.

Why does she live on the mountain?
Because she helps anybody who gets lost in the snow, that's her job.
A little goat slipped down into a crack in the ice.
And the boy leader, um, Rider, called on his watch, said this looks like a job for the pups.
We made a new one tonight, a snowy owl pup named Frost.
Frost can fly over the snow and find anybody with her big eyes.
So Frost flew up high and spotted the little goat way down.
And Everest used her snowboard, whoosh, down the hill.
Then what happened?
She used her cable to lift the goat up nice and slow.
And Frost flew next to it so the goat wouldn't be scared.
They all got back and got warm and dry.
And Frost got her own little pup tag with a snowflake on it.
Okay sleepy, that's enough mountain for one night.
